suvaco.meaning: soft-spoken; easy to speak to, amenable to correction
breakdown: Root “to speak”: easy to address, receptive to admonition.

suvatthi.meaning: well-being; welfare; blessing (as in the refrain “suvatthi hotu” — may there be happiness)
breakdown: Euphonic glide consonant inserted in sandhi.

suvaṇṇaṁ.gram: neuter noun / adjective (nominative/accusative singular), from vaṇṇa
breakdown: Colour, appearance (√var). Together: of fine colour — gold.

suvijāno.meaning: easy to know; well perceiving, readily understood

suññabhāvaṁ.meaning: the state of being empty; emptiness (of a dwelling, or of self)

suññāgāreva.meaning: or to an empty dwelling; likewise to an empty place (for meditation)
suññāgāreva.gram: neuter noun, compound (locative singular) + enclitic eva/va
breakdown: House, dwelling. Suññāgāra: an empty, secluded place.
suññāgāreva.goenka: The empty dwelling (suññāgāra) completes the classic triad of meditation places: “gone to the forest, to the root of a tree, or to an empty place”. The meditator seeks seclusion so that, undisturbed, he may sit down, establish awareness and begin observing the breath and the body.

suṇantassa.meaning: of/for one who is hearing; by hearing

suṇantu.meaning: may they listen; let them hear
suṇantu.gram: verb, imperative 3rd person plural of suṇāti

suṇātha.gram: verb, imperative 2nd person plural of suṇāti

svākkhāto.meaning: well proclaimed; perfectly expounded (said of the Dhamma)
breakdown: Prefix: well (su + a- gives svā- in sandhi).
svākkhāto.goenka: Svākkhāto opens the traditional recollection of the Dhamma: svākkhāto bhagavatā dhammo — “well proclaimed by the Blessed One is the Dhamma”. The teaching is complete and perfectly expounded: good in the beginning (sīla), good in the middle (samādhi) and good in the end (paññā), to be experienced directly by each meditator.

svāssa.meaning: and he… of him (so + assa, sandhi combination)
breakdown: He (nominative singular pronoun; o + a- gives vā in sandhi).
Of him / there would be (genitive pronoun, or optative of atthi, by context).

sādhakasuttaṁ.meaning: the Sādhaka Sutta, the Discourse on Accomplishing
sādhakasuttaṁ.gram: proper noun, neuter (title of a discourse)

sādhetī.gram: verb, present tense 3rd person singular of sādheti (causative of √sādh)
breakdown: Causative present ending (final vowel metrically lengthened).

sādhukaṁ.meaning: well; carefully, properly (as the lexicon's context renders: straight)

sādiyati.meaning: enjoys for oneself; accepts, agrees to
sādiyati.gram: verb, present tense 3rd person singular (middle/passive of sādeti, √sād)
breakdown: Middle/passive stem formation: to enjoy for oneself, consent.
Root “to speak, tell”: talking together, discussion.

sākuṇiko.gram: masculine agent noun (nominative singular), from sakuṇa
breakdown: Agent suffix: one who deals with birds, a fowler.

sālibījaṁ.note: divergence: the lexicon's running gloss reads “Sal tree + seed”; sāli is the fine rice plant (the sal tree is sāla), and the sutta simile concerns a rice seed.

sāmantajappā.meaning: indirect talk; roundabout speech, talking near the subject (a form of hinting practised for gain)
breakdown: Muttering, talk (√jap). Together: talk that circles the subject, insinuation.

sāmaññaphalasutta.meaning: the Sāmaññaphala Sutta, the Discourse on the Fruits of Recluseship
sāmaññaphalasutta.gram: proper noun (title of a discourse, Dīghanikāya 2)
breakdown: Recluseship, the state of a samaṇa.

sāmaññaphalāni.meaning: the fruits of recluseship; the results of the life of a samaṇa

sāmaññatthañca.gram: masculine noun, compound (accusative singular) + enclitic ca
breakdown: And. (The -ṁ becomes -ñ before c in sandhi.)

sāmaṁ.meaning: by oneself; oneself, of one's own accord

sāmīcipaṭipanno.meaning: practising properly; of correct conduct (said of the Saṅgha)
breakdown: Practising, entered on the path (past participle of paṭipajjati).
sāmīcipaṭipanno.goenka: Sāmīcipaṭipanno is the fourth quality in the recollection of the Saṅgha: the order of disciples practises properly — with supaṭipanno (practising well), ujupaṭipanno (practising straightly) and ñāyapaṭipanno (practising rightly for liberation). Conduct in accordance with the Dhamma, not mere robes or ritual, makes the Saṅgha worthy of offerings and respect.

sāpadesa.meaning: with reasons; well-founded (of speech supported by grounds)

breakdown: Abstract suffix: what belongs to the owner — property.

sāpateyyaṁpissa.meaning: and even his property; also of his wealth
sāpateyyaṁpissa.gram: sandhi fusion: noun (nominative/accusative singular) + enclitics pi + assa

sāpi.gram: sandhi fusion: pronoun sā + enclitic pi
breakdown: She, that (feminine nominative singular of ta).
sāpi.note: uncertain: the lexicon's gloss line here belongs to the adjacent compound sokaparidevadukkhadomanassupāyāsāpi (“sorrow, lamentation, pain, grief, distress + also”), of which sāpi is the final sandhi segment.

sāra.meaning: essence; the heartwood, the most valuable core
breakdown: Root: the firm, essential core; heartwood of a tree.
sāra.goenka: Sāra, essence or heartwood, figures in the Buddha's similes of the seeker who must not mistake leaves, twigs, bark or sapwood for the heartwood of the holy life. Taking the essence as essence and the non-essential as non-essential, one attains the essence — liberation itself.

sāramatino.meaning: those who see the essence (in the essence); thinking (something) to be the essence

breakdown: Root “to be coloured, excited”: impassioned, inflamed.

sāraṇīya.meaning: courteous; pleasant, friendly (of speech or dealings that promote concord)
breakdown: Formation meaning “fit to be remembered / to gladden”: polite, conducive to harmony.

sāre.meaning: in the essence; the innermost, the pith (locative)

sāriputtamoggallānapabbajjākathā, mahāva.meaning: “The account of the going-forth of Sāriputta and Moggallāna”, in the Mahāvagga text, of the Vinayapiṭaka
breakdown: The account (kathā) of the going forth (pabbajjā) of Sāriputta and Moggallāna, the two chief disciples.
The Mahāvagga text (Great Division of the Vinaya).
In the Vinayapiṭaka, the Basket of Discipline (locative).

sāsana.meaning: the teaching; the dispensation (of the Buddha); instruction, message
sāsana.goenka: Sāsana is the teaching of the Buddha as a living dispensation — not a body of doctrine to be believed but a complete training to be practised. To work within the sāsana means to walk on the path of sīla, samādhi and paññā while the teaching is still available in its purity.

sāsanapaggaha.meaning: support of the teaching; giving patronage to the sāsana

sāsanaparicchedo.meaning: the limitation of the teaching; the boundary or defined extent of the sāsana

sāsavo.meaning: accompanied by the taints; with flowing impurities (subject to the āsavas)
breakdown: Taint, mental impurity that flows (ā + √su “to flow”).

sāsavā.meaning: with taints; accompanied by flowing impurities (plural)
breakdown: Taint, impurity that flows in and intoxicates the mind.

sāssa.meaning: she… of him/to him (sā + assa, sandhi combination)
breakdown: Of/to him (genitive/dative singular). (The two vowels merge in sandhi.)

sātarūpaṁ.meaning: of pleasant appearance; an agreeable, pleasing object
breakdown: Form, object; at the end of a compound: “of such a nature”.
